source_jobs.title: Senior Project Manager - New Nuclear (Electrical)
source_jobs.description:
Current Opportunity Posting: We are actively hiring for 1 opening. Applications submitted through this posting will be considered for the current vacancy, and may also be considered for future opportunities that align with a candidate's experience and interests.




🌎 ABOUT US 

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools and lean processes to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our New Nuclear team scales this expertise through strategic project management of new nuclear projects, from concept through execution, across Canada, the U.S., and globally. 




🧠 THE ROLE 

We are seeking a Senior Project Manager (Electrical) to lead complex electrical engineering scopes within new nuclear projects. This role is responsible for overseeing the planning, execution, integration, and delivery of electrical systems across multidisciplinary reactor projects. 

You will manage the full lifecycle of electrical project scopes, from conceptual design through detailed engineering, procurement, construction support, commissioning, and turnover, ensuring delivery on time, within budget, and to regulatory and safety requirements. 

You will coordinate internal electrical engineering teams, cross-functional disciplines (mechanical, I&C, civil, licensing), clients, utilities, and external vendors while proactively managing technical risks and ensuring alignment across stakeholders. 



Travel Requirements: Requires on-site presence in Bruce County approximately three days per week to support collaboration, construction interface, and field decision-making. 

Salary range: $140-170k (Exact Compensation may vary). 




🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE 

 - Own and drive electrical design and execution packages, including power distribution systems, switchgear, transformers, protection and control systems, cable management, grounding, and integration with on-site and off-site buildings. 

 - Engage engineering teams with construction teams to intelligently plan site demolition and site preparation activities to be ready for a large-scale nuclear new build. 

 - Ensure seamless coordination between electrical systems and mechanical, I&C, and civil disciplines, resolving interface risks early and preventing downstream impacts. 

 - Support new reactor and first-of-a-kind deployments by introducing innovative electrical design approaches, modularization strategies, and digital engineering tools. 

 - Identify and mitigate technical, regulatory, and construction risks specific to nuclear electrical systems while ensuring compliance with CSA, IEEE, NEC/CEC, and nuclear regulatory standards. 

 - Manage relationships with utilities, OEMs, EPC partners, and electrical equipment suppliers to ensure technical alignment, procurement readiness, and timely delivery. 

 - Provide leadership through installation, testing, energization, and commissioning phases, ensuring electrical systems perform safely and as designed. 




✨ WHAT YOU’LL BRING 

 - Deep Electrical Engineering Expertise: Strong background in power systems, nuclear or large industrial electrical design, and system integration within complex infrastructure environments. 

 - Proven Project Leadership: Experience leading multidisciplinary project teams and managing electrical scopes through full lifecycle delivery in regulated industries. 

 - Results & Accountability: You take ownership of scope, schedule, and budget performance, driving teams toward measurable outcomes. 

 - Adaptability in Complex Environments: Comfort navigating ambiguity, evolving regulatory requirements, and first-of-a-kind project challenges. 

 - Technical Risk Foresight: Ability to anticipate integration, constructability, procurement, and commissioning risks before they impact delivery. 

 - Cultural Leadership: You model accountability, collaboration, me

source_jobs.title: Senior .NET Developer (Coming Soon!)
source_jobs.description:
Future Opportunity Posting: We are proactively building a pool of qualified candidates for anticipated hiring needs. There is no immediate opening at this time, but candidates who align with our needs may be contacted as opportunities arise.




🌍 ABOUT US

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally.




🧠 THE ROLE

As a Senior .NET Developer at NPX, you will own the design and evolution of mission-critical software systems that power digital transformation across the nuclear industry. You will operate at the intersection of application architecture, cloud infrastructure, and operational excellence, building scalable, resilient systems designed to perform in high-stakes environments. Partnering closely with product leaders, nuclear domain experts, and fellow developers, you will translate complex operational challenges into robust technical solutions. From distributed backend services to cloud-native deployments and CI/CD pipelines, you’ll think beyond individual components and design with the full system in mind.

This role requires strong ownership. You’ll lead architectural decisions, influence developer standards, and take accountability for solutions from concept through production. Success in this role means delivering software that is reliable, maintainable, and built to scale, while continuously raising the technical bar for the team.

If you’re energized by autonomy, high standards, and meaningful impact in a critical industry, you’ll thrive here.



Salary Range: $135-165k (Exact compensation may vary)




🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE

 - Own the architecture and technical direction of critical .NET systems across application and infrastructure layers.

 - Design and deliver cloud-native, distributed applications that power mission-critical nuclear industry workflows.

 - Architect for scalability, observability, and resilience — ensuring systems perform under real-world operational constraints.

 - Lead complex initiatives spanning application code, data layers, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment environments.

 - Partner with product and leadership to translate business objectives into scalable technical strategy.

 - Raise the engineering bar through rigorous code reviews, best practices, and operational excellence.

 - Proactively identify and eliminate systemic bottlenecks across code, pipelines, and environments.

 - Shape our technical roadmap across platform, reliability, and performance domains.




✨ WHAT YOU BRING

 - Full-System Ownership: You think beyond features - you consider infrastructure, reliability, and long-term maintainability.

 - Cloud & DevOps Fluency: You’re comfortable with CI/CD, containerization, cloud services, and production operations.

 - Distributed Systems Thinking: You design for scale, failure, and real-world constraints.

 - Execution Excellence: You deliver complex initiatives with measurable operational impact.

 - Technical Leadership: You influence architecture and raise engineering standards across teams.

 - Cultural Amplification: You strengthen our culture and role model our values.


🌿 WHAT YOU’LL FIND HERE

To do great work, you need to be well in mind, body, and soul. At NPX, we design our environment around the whole person, not just the job they do.

 - 🧠 Mind: Tools and support for mental wellness, professional growth, and financial security.

 - 🧘‍♀️ Body: Health coverage, a spending account, and the freedom to rest and recharge.

 - 🌀 Soul: Meaningful work, alignment with your values, and time to refresh with time off.

 - 💰👀 Financial wellbeing? Covered. T

source_jobs.title: Senior Mechanical Design Engineer (Coming Soon!)
source_jobs.description:
🌎 ABOUT US 

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to deliver innovative design engineering solutions that unlock performance. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively.  Our Design Engineer team scales this expertise through advanced electrical, mechanical, and instrumentation & control (I&C) design practices to clients across Canada. 




🧠 THE ROLE 

The Senior Mechanical Design Engineer is responsible for delivering high-quality mechanical design solutions for nuclear facilities. In this role, you will lead complex design scopes, provide technical direction and oversight, and serve as a trusted engineering partner to clients. 

You will apply mechanical design engineering expertise to guide projects from concept through detailed design, while mentoring junior engineers, driving continuous improvement in design processes, and championing innovation in how nuclear design engineering work is executed. 



Travel Requirements: Occasional travel to the client site in Bruce County, as required by project needs.

Salary Range: $130-165k (Exact compensation may vary).




🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE 

 - Provide technical leadership and engineering judgment, including defining design approaches, resolving complex technical issues, and ensuring alignment with safety, regulatory, and project requirements. 

 - Prepare, review, and approve mechanical engineering deliverables in accordance with applicable procedures, codes, standards, and regulatory requirements. 

 - Act as a technical interface with clients, vendors, regulators, and internal stakeholders, supporting design reviews, technical meetings, and issue resolution. 

 - Collaborate across disciplines to define and achieve the required functionality, performance, reliability, availability, and maintainability of systems and components. 

 - Support and mentor junior engineers by providing guidance, design oversight, and constructive technical feedback. 

 - Work closely with QA and project teams to address design issues, support audits and reviews, and ensure overall design quality. 

 - Contribute to the evolution of NPX’s design tools, standards, and processes, identifying opportunities to improve efficiency, consistency, and technical excellence. 




✨ WHAT YOU BRING  

 - Results & Impact: you deliver measurable outcomes and business value.  

 - Leadership & Influence: you elevate team performance and create influence without being asked.  

 - Adaptability & Growth: you thrive in dynamic environments and embody a growth mindset.  

 - Initiative & Innovation: you proactively identify and solve problems.  

 - Cultural Amplification: you strengthen our culture and role model our values.   


🌿 WHAT YOU’LL FIND HERE

To do great work, you need to be well in mind, body, and soul. At NPX, we design our environment around the whole person, not just the job they do.

 - 🧠 Mind: Tools and support for mental wellness, professional growth, and financial security.

 - 🧘‍♀️ Body: Health coverage, a spending account, and the freedom to rest and recharge.

 - 🌀 Soul: Meaningful work, alignment with your values, and time to refresh with time off.

 - 💰👀 Financial wellbeing? Covered. That means competitive pay and recognition for the impact you make.



NPX provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression. We are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce and welcome people from all backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and abilities.

source_jobs.title: Senior DevOps Developer (Coming Soon!)
source_jobs.description:
Future Opportunity Posting: We are proactively building a pool of qualified candidates for anticipated hiring needs. There is no immediate opening at this time, but candidates who align with our needs may be contacted as opportunities arise.




🌍 ABOUT US 

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally.  




🧠 THE ROLE  

We’re seeking a hands-on Senior DevOps Developer who thrives on designing, building, and operating secure, production-grade cloud and on-premises infrastructure in regulated environments. You’ll play a key role in enabling our engineering teams to deploy mission-critical digital solutions into the nuclear industry. 

This role sits at the intersection of cloud architecture, DevSecOps, CI/CD, and reliability engineering. You’ll own infrastructure strategy, drive automation at scale, and ensure our SaaS and AI-enabled systems are secure, resilient, and production-ready. 

If you enjoy taking an idea from whiteboard → working code → secure, compliant production system - this role is for you. 



Travel Requirements: Travel for quarterly team events.

Salary Range: $170-190k (Exact compensation may vary)




🎯WHAT YOU'LL DRIVE 

 - Architect, implement, and maintain secure cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, or GCP, Azure is preferred) supporting scalable SaaS and AI-enabled products. 

 - Lead containerization and orchestration efforts using Docker and Kubernetes (AKS/EKS/GKE, AKS is preferred). 

 - Design, build, and optimize CI/CD pipelines incorporating automated testing, security scanning, and deployment controls (DevSecOps). 

 - Develop and maintain infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, ARM, or equivalent) to ensure reproducible, auditable environments. 

 - Establish observability standards (metrics, logging, tracing) using modern monitoring tools to support SLIs/SLOs and high availability targets. 

 - Own reliability engineering practices including disaster recovery strategies, backup automation, and incident response processes. 

 - Implement secure architecture patterns, IAM controls, secrets management, and infrastructure hardening suitable for regulated industries. 

 - Collaborate closely with software engineers to embed DevOps best practices directly into product development. 

 - Contribute to architectural decisions and define cloud platform standards that scale across client deployments. 

 - Help raise the bar for engineering quality, security, and operational excellence across the team. 

 - Lead post-incident sessions for when production issues occur and root-cause analysis is required.




✨WHAT YOU BRING 

 - Technical Breadth: You understand how software is built, deployed, and operated. A background in application development is a strong asset, enabling deeper troubleshooting, better automation, and more effective partnership with product teams. 

 - Results & Impact: You deliver secure, reliable, and scalable infrastructure that drives measurable product and customer value. 

 - Hands-On Leadership: You lead by building, setting technical standards through code, architecture, and operational excellence. 

 - Adaptability & Growth: You continuously learn and apply modern cloud, automation, and DevSecOps practices that perform in real production environments. 

 - Initiative & Ownership: You take systems from concept to compliant, production-ready deployment and own outcomes end-to-end. 

 - Cultural Amplification: You elevate the team through collaboration, knowledge sharing, and a high bar for craftsmanship and reliability. 
   
   


🌿 WHAT YOU’LL FIND HERE

To do great

source_jobs.title: Senior AI Developer (Coming Soon!)
source_jobs.description:
Future Opportunity Posting: We are proactively building a pool of qualified candidates for anticipated hiring needs. There is no immediate opening at this time, but candidates who align with our needs may be contacted as opportunities arise.




🌎 ABOUT US 

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operations expertise with AI, digital tools, and Lean processes to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. 

As an innovation partner, we’re scaling this expertise into production-grade SaaS products, bringing our solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally. 





🧠 THE ROLE

We’re looking for a hands-on Senior AI Developer who enjoys both designing systems and implementing them in production code. You’ll play a key role in building and shipping enterprise-grade AI products under our SaaS product, a platform designed to automate nuclear workflows to support efficient operations for a sustainable future in the industry. This role sits at the intersection of AI system design, backend engineering, and real-world delivery. 

If you enjoy taking an idea from whiteboard → working code → deployed system, this role is for you. 



Tech Stack: React, Next.js, Python, Azure, Minio, Weaviate, Ollama, Postgres, Redis 

Salary Range: $130–160k (exact compensation may vary). 




🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE 

 - Design end-to-end AI system architectures (e.g., Agent-based systems) and implement core components in production code. 

 - Build, test, and maintain retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines, including GraphRAG and emerging approaches (e.g. OmniRAG). 

 - Develop backend services, APIs, and data pipelines that integrate AI models into real workflows. 

 - Write and maintain Python code daily, using Azure’s AI SDK and cloud-native services. 

 - Implement AI-powered workflows such as: 

 - Document digitization and classification 

 - Data vectorization and semantic search 

 - Content generation and summarization 

 - Prepare, structure, and validate training and evaluation datasets with production constraints in mind. 

 - Contribute to technical discussions, code reviews, and architectural decisions. 

 - Help raise the bar for engineering quality across the team through example. 




✨ WHAT YOU BRING 

 - Results & Impact: You ship working software that delivers measurable value. 

 - Hands-On Leadership: You lead by building, not just by advising. 

 - Adaptability & Growth: You enjoy learning and applying new tools that work in production. 

 - Initiative & Ownership: You take ideas from concept to completion. 

 - Cultural Amplification: You strengthen the team through collaboration and craftsmanship 


🌿 WHAT YOU’LL FIND HERE

To do great work, you need to be well in mind, body, and soul. At NPX, we design our environment around the whole person, not just the job they do.

 - 🧠 Mind: Tools and support for mental wellness, professional growth, and financial security.

 - 🧘‍♀️ Body: Health coverage, a spending account, and the freedom to rest and recharge.

 - 🌀 Soul: Meaningful work, alignment with your values, and time to refresh with time off.

 - 💰👀 Financial wellbeing? Covered. That means competitive pay and recognition for the impact you make.



NPX provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression. We are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce and welcome people from all backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and abilities.



💬 Pssst. Just a heads up that we use AI to help in the filtering of candidates based on job-specific criteria. Humans decide who to move forward, and this way we're able to spend more of our time supporting our candidates directly.

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
Current Opportunity Posting: We are actively hiring for 1 opening. Applications submitted through this posting will be considered for the current vacancy, and may also be considered for future opportunities that align with a candidate's experience and interests.




🌍 ABOUT US

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally.  




🧠 THE ROLE 

We’re looking for a Senior Full Stack Developer to help build the future of digital solutions at NPX. You’ll play a critical role in shaping products that make nuclear safer, faster, and more cost-effective. 

We’re looking for someone who has: 

 - Led the development of a full-stack application from scratch. 

 - Built on top of an existing application to set it up to scale across clients. 

 - Deep expertise in React, JavaScript, TypeScript, Express, and Node.js, and can use this to design intuitive front-end experiences and scalable back-end systems.  

 - A curiosity and interest in AI: you don’t need to be an expert, but you’re eager to experiment and learn. 

If you’re driven by impact, thrive in a fast-moving environment, and want to help shape the digital future of nuclear, we’d love to meet you. 

Salary Range: $140-190k (Exact compensation may vary).   




🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE  

 - The development of an application from scratch, and building on top of an existing application, to set our products up to scale across multiple clients in the nuclear industry. 

 - The implementation of new features and integrations to enhance the application’s capabilities. 

 - Partner with engineers, designers, and analysts to solve complex technical challenges and improve application performance. 

 - Proactively identify and improve code quality, system performance, and scalability to make the platform more reliable and maintainable. 

 - Translate complex technical challenges into actionable plans, ensuring the platform continues to support safer, faster, and more cost-effective nuclear operations.

source_jobs.description:

🌍 ABOUT US

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally. 




🧠 THE ROLE 

We’re looking for a Project Delivery Manager to lead the successful delivery of digital projects in the nuclear industry. You’ll drive end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution, ensuring projects are delivered on schedule, within scope, and with high quality. You’ll collaborate with our Strategy, Product Development and Operations teams including product managers, business analysts, UI/UX designers, software developers, testers, change management practitioners and client stakeholders to translate requirements into clear delivery plans and ensure our teams are aligned and supported throughout the development lifecycle. In some cases, we also support the implementation of off-the-shelf solutions, serving as the bridge between utilities and vendor partners.

 - Project management of technical scopes related to nuclear projects, ensuring work is completed on time, on budget, and to quality expectations.

 - Develop, track, and maintain integrated project schedules, budgets, and forecasts using modern project management tools (e.g., Microsoft Projects, Azure DevOps, Smartsheet).

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Manager - New Nuclear (Civil/Earthworks)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US 

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools and lean processes to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our New Nuclear team scales this expertise through strategic project management of new nuclear projects, from concept through execution, across Canada, the U.S., and globally. 


THE ROLE 

We are seeking a Senior Project Manager (Civil / Earthworks) to lead large-scale site development and heavy civil scopes supporting new nuclear projects. This role will oversee the planning, coordination, and execution of earthworks, grading, excavation, foundations, underground utilities, concrete structures, and enabling works required for reactor construction. 

You will manage the full lifecycle of civil project scopes, from early site preparation and constructability planning through execution and turnover, ensuring delivery on time, within budget, and in strict compliance with nuclear safety and regulatory standards. 

You will coordinate civil engineering teams, geotechnical consultants, construction contractors, utilities, and cross-disciplinary stakeholders (electrical, mechanical, I&C) while proactively managing risks related to site conditions, constructability, logistics, and environmental compliance. 

Travel Requirements: Requires on-site presence in Bruce County approximately three days per week to support construction coordination, contractor oversight, and field decision-making. 

Salary Range: $140-170k (Exact compensation may vary) 


WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE 

 - Oversee grading, excavation, soil stabilization, foundation works, structural concrete, drainage systems, underground services, and access infrastructure required for new reactor builds. 

 - Identify and mitigate site-specific risks including soil conditions, groundwater management, environmental compliance, and weather impacts that can influence schedule and cost. 

 - Develop and optimize construction sequencing plans that align civil works with mechanical, electrical, and structural installation milestones to avoid downstream delays. 

 - Manage EPC partners, heavy civil contractors, and subcontractors to ensure quality, safety, productivity, and adherence to nuclear-grade standards. 

 - Ensure all civil works meet CSA, nuclear regulatory requirements, environmental permits, and safety standards while maintaining rigorous documentation and quality controls. 

 - Proactively manage budgets, forecasts, contingency planning, and schedule risk for large-scale infrastructure scopes with significant capital impact. 

 - Support innovative reactor deployments by introducing modularization strategies, digital construction tools, and lean site management practices that redefine how nuclear sites are built. 


WHAT YOU’LL BRING 

 - Deep Civil & Earthworks Expertise: Strong experience in heavy civil construction, large infrastructure projects, or nuclear/industrial site development including foundations and underground utilities. 

 - Proven Construction Leadership: Demonstrated ability to lead multidisciplinary teams and contractors through complex site execution in regulated or safety-critical environments. 

 - Risk & Site Awareness: Strong understanding of geotechnical challenges, constructability constraints, environmental considerations, and large-scale project logistics. 

 - Results & Accountability: You take ownership of site performance, schedule, cost, safety, and quality, and drive teams toward measurable outcomes.

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Manager - New Nuclear (Electrical)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US 

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools and lean processes to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our New Nuclear team scales this expertise through strategic project management of new nuclear projects, from concept through execution, across Canada, the U.S., and globally. 


THE ROLE 

We are seeking a Senior Project Manager (Electrical) to lead complex electrical engineering scopes within new nuclear projects. This role is responsible for overseeing the planning, execution, integration, and delivery of electrical systems across multidisciplinary reactor projects. 

You will manage the full lifecycle of electrical project scopes, from conceptual design through detailed engineering, procurement, construction support, commissioning, and turnover, ensuring delivery on time, within budget, and to regulatory and safety requirements. 

You will coordinate internal electrical engineering teams, cross-functional disciplines (mechanical, I&C, civil, licensing), clients, utilities, and external vendors while proactively managing technical risks and ensuring alignment across stakeholders. 

Travel Requirements: Requires on-site presence in Bruce County approximately three days per week to support collaboration, construction interface, and field decision-making. 

Salary range: $140-170k (Exact Compensation may vary). 


WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE 

 - Own and drive electrical design and execution packages, including power distribution systems, switchgear, transformers, protection and control systems, cable management, grounding, and integration with on-site and off-site buildings. 

 - Engage engineering teams with construction teams to intelligently plan site demolition and site preparation activities to be ready for a large-scale nuclear new build. 

 - Ensure seamless coordination between electrical systems and mechanical, I&C, and civil disciplines, resolving interface risks early and preventing downstream impacts. 

 - Support new reactor and first-of-a-kind deployments by introducing innovative electrical design approaches, modularization strategies, and digital engineering tools. 

 - Identify and mitigate technical, regulatory, and construction risks specific to nuclear electrical systems while ensuring compliance with CSA, IEEE, NEC/CEC, and nuclear regulatory standards. 

 - Manage relationships with utilities, OEMs, EPC partners, and electrical equipment suppliers to ensure technical alignment, procurement readiness, and timely delivery. 

 - Provide leadership through installation, testing, energization, and commissioning phases, ensuring electrical systems perform safely and as designed. 


WHAT YOU’LL BRING 

 - Deep Electrical Engineering Expertise: Strong background in power systems, nuclear or large industrial electrical design, and system integration within complex infrastructure environments. 

 - Proven Project Leadership: Experience leading multidisciplinary project teams and managing electrical scopes through full lifecycle delivery in regulated industries. 

 - Results & Accountability: You take ownership of scope, schedule, and budget performance, driving teams toward measurable outcomes. 

 - Adaptability in Complex Environments: Comfort navigating ambiguity, evolving regulatory requirements, and first-of-a-kind project challenges. 

 - Technical Risk Foresight: Ability to anticipate integration, constructability, procurement, and commissioning risks before they impact delivery. 

 - Cultural Leadership: You model accountability, collaboration, mentorship, and innovation, strengthening team culture while delivering technical excellence. 

 - Stakeholder Management: You are able to interface with a wide range of stakeholders inside nuclear and beyond to municipalities, other corporations, and government bodies. 


🌿 WHAT YOU’

source_jobs.title: Design Engineering Intern
source_jobs.description:
Current Opportunity Posting: We are actively hiring for 2 openings. Applications submitted through this posting will be considered for the current vacancies and may also be considered for future opportunities that align with a candidate's experience and interests.




🌍 ABOUT US

NPX partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Design Engineering team scales this expertise through advanced electrical, mechanical, and instrumentation & control (I&C) design practices to clients across Canada.




🧠 THE ROLE

A Design Engineering Intern will gain hands on experience across a variety of nuclear design engineering projects. You’ll help resolve emergent issues, perform engineering calculations and analyses, support maintenance and procurement activities, and assist with minor design changes. You will gain meaningful, real-world experience, while applying classroom knowledge to practical challenges in the nuclear industry. You’ll bring curiosity, creativity, and problem-solving skills to a team that values fresh perspectives and innovative thinking.

Depending on your background and areas of interest, the role may align with either the Mechanical Design team or the Electrical & I&C Design team.




🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE

 - Collaborate with design engineers and stakeholders to develop high‑quality designs and review supporting documentation, technical specs, codes, standards, and test/implementation plans.

 - Participate in plant walk‑downs to assess equipment and gather design inputs.

 - Prepare and present technical information to internal and external audiences.

 - Support daily project activities, coordinating with stakeholders to ensure deliverables meet quality expectations.

 - Contribute to innovation and technical advancement projects, including research into new technologies and their potential applications in the nuclear industry.

 - Incorporate lessons learned into processes to strengthen future design work.




📝 LOGISTICS

 - Location: Hybrid

 - Start: Fall Term (September)

 - Duration: 8-, 12-, or 16 – month

 - Salary Range: $26-31/hour (depending on the academic level)

 - Travel Requirements: Occasional travel to the client site in Bruce County, as required by project needs.

 - Condition of Employment: All NPXers are required to obtain a nuclear security clearance as a condition of employment. We'd help you every step of the way but wanted to let you know!




✨ WHAT YOU BRING

 - A background in Mechanical, Chemical, or Electrical Engineering.

 - Results & Impact: you deliver measurable outcomes and business value.

 - Leadership & Influence: you elevate team performance and create influence without being asked.

 - Adaptability & Growth: you thrive in dynamic environments and embody a growth mindset.

 - Initiative & Innovation: you proactively identify and solve problems.

 - Cultural Amplification: you strengthen our culture and role model our values.

To do great work, you need to be well in mind, body, and soul. At NPX, we design our environment around the whole person, not just the job they do.

 - 🧠 Mind: Tools and support for mental wellness, professional growth, and financial security.

 - 🧘‍♀️ Body: Health coverage, a spending account, and the freedom to rest and recharge.

 - 🌀 Soul: Meaningful work, alignment with your values, and time to refresh with time off.

 - 💰👀 Financial wellbeing? Covered. That means competitive pay and recognition for the impact you make.



NPX provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression. source_jobs.title: AI Developer Co-op
source_jobs.description:
🌍 ABOUT US 

NPX  https://www.npxinnovation.ca/partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our AI Products & Services team scales this expertise through NPX-developed AI products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally. 




🧠 THE ROLE  

NPX is seeking an AI Developer co-op to design, develop, and deploy AI agents and contribute to our AI products that revolutionize how work is being done in Nuclear. 

Your work will improve the efficiency of nuclear operations, contributing to safer, faster, and more cost-effective outcomes across the nuclear industry. This role is for someone who thrives on taking ownership of projects, juggling multiple initiatives at once, and quickly learning new tools and technologies, particularly in AI. 

This role could be a mix of AI development & full-stack development depending on your interests and experience. 

Location:  Remote from Ontario 

Start Date:  May 4, 2026  

Duration: 4 month to start with potential to extend 

Salary Range:  $26-31/hour (depending on the academic level)  

Travel Requirements: Potential travel to office 2-3 times throughout the co-op term

Condition of Employment: All NPXers are required to obtain a nuclear security clearance as a condition of employment. We'd help you every step of the way but wanted to let you know!

 


🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE  

 - Design, develop, and deploy AI agents that solve real problems and enhance user experiences. 

 - Own and develop new features for our products and build upon existing functionality. 

 - Support the transition from prototypes to production by contributing to front-end/back-end integration and basic testing. 

 - Continuously and rapidly refine ideas through feedback, data, and ongoing experimentation. 

Tech Stack: React, Next.js, Python, Azure, Minio, Weaviate, Ollama, Postgres, Redis   

 


🌿 WHAT YOU’LL FIND HERE

To do great work, you need to be well in mind, body, and soul. At NPX, we design our environment around the whole person, not just the job they do.

 - 🧠 Mind: Tools and support for mental wellness, professional growth, and financial security.

 - 🧘‍♀️ Body: Health coverage, a spending account, and the freedom to rest and recharge.

 - 🌀 Soul: Meaningful work, alignment with your values, and time to refresh with time off.

 - 💰👀 Financial wellbeing? Covered. That means competitive pay and recognition for the impact you make.



NPX provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression. We are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce and welcome people from all backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and abilities.

source_jobs.title: Senior .NET Developer
source_jobs.description:
🌍 ABOUT US

NPX partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally.




🧠 THE ROLE

As a Senior .NET Developer at NPX you will own the design and evolution of mission-critical software systems that power digital transformation across the nuclear industry. You will operate at the intersection of application architecture, cloud infrastructure, and operational excellence, building scalable, resilient systems designed to perform in high-stakes environments. Partnering closely with product leaders, nuclear domain experts, and fellow developers, you will translate complex operational challenges into robust technical solutions. From distributed backend services to cloud-native deployments and CI/CD pipelines, you’ll think beyond individual components and design with the full system in mind.

This role requires strong ownership. You’ll lead architectural decisions, influence developer standards, and take accountability for solutions from concept through production. Success in this role means delivering software that is reliable, maintainable, and built to scale, while continuously raising the technical bar for the team.

If you’re energized by autonomy, high standards, and meaningful impact in a critical industry, you’ll thrive here.



Work Location Requirement: This role requires on-site presence in Chalk River, one day per week at most for deployments into production.



Security Clearance: Please note that this role requires Level 2 security clearance. Candidates must have resided in Canada for a minimum of seven (7) consecutive years to qualify.




🎯 WHAT YOU'LL DRIVE

 - Own the architecture and technical direction of critical .NET systems across application and infrastructure layers.

 - Design and deliver cloud-native, distributed applications that power mission-critical nuclear industry workflows.

 - Architect for scalability, observability, and resilience — ensuring systems perform under real-world operational constraints.

 - Lead complex initiatives spanning application code, data layers, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment environments.

 - Partner with product and leadership to translate business objectives into scalable technical strategy.

 - Raise the engineering bar through rigorous code reviews, best practices, and operational excellence.

 - Proactively identify and eliminate systemic bottlenecks across code, pipelines, and environments.

 - Shape our technical roadmap across platform, reliability, and performance domains.




✨ WHAT YOU BRING

 - Full-System Ownership: You think beyond features - you consider infrastructure, reliability, and long-term maintainability.

 - Cloud & DevOps Fluency: You’re comfortable with CI/CD, containerization, cloud services, and production operations.

 - Distributed Systems Thinking: You design for scale, failure, and real-world constraints.

 - Execution Excellence: You deliver complex initiatives with measurable operational impact.

 - Technical Leadership: You influence architecture and raise engineering standards across teams.

 - Cultural Amplification: You strengthen our culture and role model our values.

🌍 ABOUT US 

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally. 




🧠 THE ROLE 

NPX is seeking a Nuclear Business Consultant to strengthen our nuclear strategy and business context across digital and AI initiatives in Canada. This role exists to bridge deep nuclear industry knowledge with business and market insight, ensuring our digital, monitoring, and AI solutions are grounded in real utility needs and positioned for growth. 

You will operate as an overarching, cross-functional advisor, supporting initiatives across the Digital Transformation and AI portfolio. Rather than being embedded in a single team, you will float where needed, bringing nuclear credibility, strategic clarity, and business perspective to help teams level up and scale impact. 



Travel Requirements: Occasional travel to the client site in Bruce County, as required by project needs.

source_jobs.title: Data Analytics Lead
source_jobs.description:
Current Opportunity Posting: We are actively hiring for 1 opening. Applications submitted through this posting will be considered for the current vacancy, and may also be considered for future opportunities that align with a candidate's experience and interests.




🌍 ABOUT US

NPX https://www.npx.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally.




🚀 THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Data Analytics Lead who can bridge the gap between technical data work and client-facing consulting. In this role, you’ll be embedded with clients, understanding their problems from the inside, shaping analytics strategies, and leading a team to deliver on them.

You’ll own the analytics function: the processes, tools, architecture, people, and the client relationships. You should be equally comfortable building a Power BI dashboard, designing a data pipeline in Azure Data Factory, and sitting across from a VP explaining what their data is telling them.

This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, analytics delivery, people leadership, and client advisory. If you want to shape how a growing company uses data to drive outcomes for the nuclear industry, this is the role.



Travel Requirement: Travel to client site approximately 4-6 times per month, as required by project needs.

Salary Range: $130-150k (Exact compensation may vary)

Number of Openings: 1




🎯 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE

 - Lead and grow a team of data analysts, setting standards for quality, delivery, and professional development.

 - Work directly with clients to understand their business problems, translate them into analytics solutions, and deliver measurable outcomes.

 - Design and build analytics solutions using Power BI, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, and the broader Azure data ecosystem.

 - Own the data architecture and infrastructure decisions for analytics engagements, from ingestion and transformation to visualization and reporting.

 - Advise clients on their digital transformation journey as it relates to data: what to modernize, what to build, and what to leave alone.

 - Establish repeatable frameworks and best practices that the team can scale across engagements.

 - Identify opportunities to deepen client relationships and expand the scope of analytics work.

 - Partner with engineering and product teams to ensure analytics infrastructure integrates cleanly with NPX’s broader digital solutions.




🧠 WHAT YOU BRING

 - Technical Depth: You’ve built and deployed Power BI reports at scale - data modeling, DAX, row-level security, deployment pipelines. You understand Databricks (notebooks, Spark, Delta Lake), Azure Data Factory orchestration, and how the Azure data ecosystem fits together (ADLS, Synapse, Purview, Unity Catalog).

 - Architecture & Infrastructure: You understand the infrastructure and architectural patterns behind enterprise data platforms - lakehouse design, medallion layers, data mesh concepts - and can make practical decisions about what to build for a given client’s needs and maturity.

 - People Leadership: You’ve led, mentored, and grown a team of analysts or data professionals. You build culture around quality, ownership, and continuous improvement, not just task assignment.

 - Consulting Mindset: You can walk into a client environment, quickly understand the business context, and identify where analytics can create real value. You translate ambiguous problems into structured approaches and communicate clearly with executives, SMEs, and technical teams alike.

 - Results & Impact: You deliver analytics solutions that drive measurable client outcomes, not j

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Current Opportunity Posting: We are actively hiring for 1 opening. Applications submitted through this posting will be considered for the current vacancy, and may also be considered for future opportunities that align with a candidate's experience and interests.




📃 ABOUT YOUR LEAD

Hi 👋, I’m Anthony https://www.linkedin.com/in/anthonypage/, Product Management Lead at NPX – your future lead!

I have spent over 15 years building and improving customer experiences, first across Europe and now in Canada. Before discovering my passion for software product management, I launched a service brand and implemented a Net Promoter Score (NPS) framework across four countries.

After two years as an individual contributor, I moved into people management, where I now mentor Product Managers who care deeply about their craft and are committed to raising the bar for how we practice product management as a team.

I can't wait to help you succeed in this role and wherever your aspirations take you. I also believe some of our best work happens when we are in the same room, so let me know if you want to grab a co-working space and a whiteboard.




🌍 ABOUT NPX

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our Digital Transformation team scales this expertise through both NPX-developed and industry-leading products, delivering solutions to clients across Canada, the U.S., and globally.




🚀 THE ROLE

We are seeking a Senior Product Manager to work closely with clients, software engineers, and designers to understand complex problems, define clear outcomes, and deliver software that advances the nuclear industry. Your work will span customer-driven project-based initiatives and/or product-based development that benefits multiple customers. In both cases, you’ll be accountable for turning validated customer needs into high-quality software that meets nuclear regulatory requirements and NPX’s high standards for safety and performance.



Travel Expectations: Occasional travel (approximately once a month).

Salary Range: $115-140k (Exact compensation may vary)




🎯 WHAT YOU'LL DRIVE

 1. Product Discovery

 - Lead discovery workshops and user interviews to uncover problems worth solving and opportunities worth pursuing

 - Partner with customers and internal stakeholders to understand workflows, constraints, and success criteria

 - Translate insights into clear, well-documented requirements that teams can execute against

 2. Product Delivery

 - Lead a cross‑functional team of engineers and designers from problem definition through delivery

 - Own scope, priorities, and trade-offs to ensure solutions meet customer needs and business goals

 3. Product Strategy

 - Identify patterns across customer work that can inform product strategy and future investments

 - When working on software products, contribute to longer-term planning and roadmap thinking




✨ WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Results & Impact: You set objectives that will blow customers’ minds, and align stakeholders towards those goals

 - Adaptability & Growth: You thrive in a dynamic environment and are passionate about deepening your product craft and understanding of the nuclear industry.

 - Initiative & Innovation: you proactively identify and solve problems.

 - Cultural Amplification: You embody and strengthen our culture and values by giving constructive feedback to teammates and stakeholders, treating every day like it’s Day 1, pursuing outcomes that blow our customers' minds, and going above and beyond to make the people around you feel supported.




🌸 YOUR INTERVIEW JOURNEY

Our interview process is designed for you to showcase your relevant experience, while gaining a real sense of who we are and how we work.

Here's what that looks like:

source_jobs.title: Senior Program Manager - New Nuclear
source_jobs.description:
Current Opportunity Posting: We are actively hiring for 3 openings. Applications submitted through this posting will be considered for the current vacancies and may also be considered for future opportunities that align with a candidate's experience and interests.



📃 About your Lead

Hi 👋, I'm Steve https://www.linkedin.com/in/stevefernandes-2a928256/, co-founder and Director of New Nuclear at NPX – your future lead! I've built my career delivering complex nuclear projects including 15+ years in field work at Bruce Power, leading multi-disciplinary teams through major refurbishment campaigns, and strategic and programmatic support to New Build Projects.

I'm a mentor, a teacher, and (full disclosure) a champion of dad jokes. I believe culture is built on smiles and being there for each other — and that's exactly the kind of team we've grown at NPX. If that sounds like your kind of place, I'd love to meet you. Let's build something great together. 🚀



🌎 About Us

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to unlock performance through innovation. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools and lean processes to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively. Our New Nuclear team scales this expertise through strategic project management of new nuclear projects, from concept through execution, across Canada, the U.S., and globally.



🧠 The Role

NPX is seeking a Senior Program Manager who is interested in shaping the future of new nuclear at NPX and across North America. The role will include leading the planning, coordination, and delivery of new nuclear projects from early development through execution. Your day-to-day will be a mixture of partnering with clients, leading the team to deliver on commitments, and working on growth across NPX’s New Build portfolio. When complex, multi-stakeholder programs are managed with discipline and clarity, Canada's nuclear future gets built on time, on budget, and to the highest safety standards — and you'll be at the centre of making that happen.



Travel Requirements: Weekly Travel to client site in Bruce County an asset, willing to relocate is a strong asset.

Salary Range: $ 160-200k (Exact compensation may vary)



Number of Openings: 3

Each opening varies in scope:

 1. AP1000 Senior Program Manager: This role leads program management for AP1000 reactor planning and deployment.

 2. Monark Senior Program Manager: This role leads program management for Monark reactor planning and deployment.

 3. Governance Senior Program Manager: This role leads program management related to the governance of new nuclear builds.
    

🎯 What You'll Drive

 - Own end-to-end program delivery — managing scope, schedule, cost, and risk to keep commitments to clients and stakeholders intact.

 - Drive proactive risk identification and mitigation across the program lifecycle, translating uncertainty into structured action plans before issues become project impacts.

 - Serve as a trusted client interface at site to build strong relationships that keep work flowing and surface what’s coming next.



✨ What You Bring

 - Results & Impact: you define what "done" looks like and drive your team to get there — on time, on budget, and without cutting corners on safety or quality.

 - Leadership & Influence: you align multi-disciplinary teams around shared goals and create accountability across organizational boundaries. Experience leading and developing people a strong asset.

 - Adaptability & Growth: you thrive in fast-moving environments and bring structure without rigidity when complexity increases.

 - Initiative & Ownership: you anticipate problems before they surface and treat your program's success as your personal responsibility.

 - Cultural Amplification: you model the discipline and care that nuclear demands — and you bring your team along with you, because program culture is safety culture.

 - Domain Familia

📃 ABOUT YOUR LEAD

Hi 👋, I’m Tam https://www.linkedin.com/in/tam-jeffs-p-eng-72a68a2/, Management Systems Director at NPX – your future lead!

I am currently leading Management Systems and Nuclear QA at NPX. My background spans engineering, project management, and quality assurance across major nuclear projects. My passion is building systems and teams that work well in practice, not just on paper. I am looking forward to adding someone to our team who brings strong QA fundamentals and a proactive mindset to continually improve our systems and processes and provide support to other companies entering nuclear who require management systems.

I cannot wait to help you succeed in this role and wherever your aspirations take you.




🌎 ABOUT US

NPX https://www.npxinnovation.ca/ partners with nuclear utilities and new reactor projects to deliver innovative design engineering solutions that unlock performance. We combine decades of nuclear operation expertise with AI, digital tools, and lean processes, to help plants run safer, smarter, and more cost-effectively.

Our Quality Assurance team scales this expertise through rigorous QA programs, audits, and continuous improvement practices that help nuclear utilities across Canada maintain the highest standards of safety and regulatory compliance.




🧠 THE ROLE

We are seeking a QA Specialist to support the execution and continuous improvement of our Nuclear Quality Management System (NQMS). In this role, you will lead by example, helping to inspire those around you while supporting the business in achieving its quality goals. You will contribute by carrying out compliance activities, supporting audits, managing non-conformance workflows, and keeping quality documentation up to date. Through this work, you will play a vital role in ensuring NPX maintains the standards our nuclear clients require.



Travel Requirements: Occasional travel to client site in Bruce County as required by client needs.

Salary Range: $100-130k (Exact compensation may vary)




🎯 WHAT YOU WILL DRIVE

 - Execute day-to-day compliance activities, draft document revisions, and track conformance to standards so nothing slips through the cracks.

 - Plan, schedule, and support internal and external audits end-to-end by preparing the documents, coordinating stakeholders, and following findings through to resolution.

 - Identify and document quality issues, recommend practical solutions, verify they have been implemented, and ensure any nonconforming work is carefully reviewed and approved before moving forward.

 - Monitor quality performance against targets, pull together the data, and share clear, useful insights with the QA Director and leadership so everyone understands where things stand.

 - Support and maintain our NQMS systems (like NPX’s QA App), keep records up to date, help users navigate the tools, and coordinate updates to QA manuals for clients.

 - Deliver and coordinate QA training, keep materials current, and track completion to make sure everyone understands expectations.

 - Review and log client feedback, assess whether it points to a quality issue, escalate when needed, and feed those learnings into corrective actions to prevent repeat issues.

 - Help create an environment where people feel comfortable raising concerns, flagging risks early, and reinforce a strong nuclear safety mindset in day-to-day work.

 - Leverage technology solutions to further improve our support.

 - Help expand our Nuclear QA as a service offering and deliver effective and efficient results to bring value to our customers.

 - Have fun knowing you make a difference!
